title: Recall Better: - Test Yourself Often: After Reading, Close the Book and Write Down
subtitle: Boost Memory with Self-Testing
description:
To recall better:
- Test yourself often: After reading, close the book and write down what you remember.
- Use flashcards: Create questions for key points and quiz yourself regularly.
- Rewrite, don’t reread: Summarize content in your own words instead of passively reviewing it.

Example: If studying for an exam, write down key concepts from memory rather than rereading the textbook.

faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify the number of items you can recall from your reading material before implementing active recall techniques. This will help measure improvement.

howTo.title: Implement active recall sessions
howTo.summary: After reading, close the book and write down everything you remember for 3–5 minutes. Create 1–3 test questions based on your recall.

howTo.title: Track your progress
howTo.summary: Log the number of items recalled and the time spent on each recall session. This will help you see your improvement over time.

howTo.title: Review and adjust techniques
howTo.summary: Evaluate your recall results and adjust your study techniques as needed. Focus on areas where you struggled to recall information.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and reinforce your learning. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/active-recall-test-yourself/.
